title: Tessera
description: A Wave Function Collapse studio — a from-scratch constraint solver with both the tiled and the canonical overlapping model, seven hand-authored tilesets plus patterns learnt live from a bitmap you draw. Steer it: inspect any cell's possibilities with the Lens, paint hand constraints and watch WFC fill the rest, re-bias tile weights, and export as PNG, WebM or JSON. NEW — a genuine GLOBAL CONNECTIVITY CONSTRAINT, the research-grade extension of WFC: layered soundly on top of the fast support-counter solver and its backtracking, it guarantees the output's large-scale shape, not just local adjacency. Two modes — 'Whole network' forces every connector cell (rail, wire, pipe, corridor) into ONE connected component, and 'Route between pins' guarantees the cells you paint are linked, growing a guaranteed-solvable maze between two endpoints. It works in three sound layers: optimistic 'could-still-link' feasibility pruning that turns an unroutable partial state into a real contradiction the solver backtracks on; exact forced-connector inference (a cell on EVERY route between two terminals — found via articulation points + a removal test — must be a connector, so its blank tiles are banned and propagated, steering the search to a connected answer); and a final validation so the solver can never report a finished grid that violates the property. Ships a from-scratch graph engine (connected components, Tarjan articulation points, multi-terminal reachability, s–t cut vertices) and a new Maze tileset built for routing. Certified the house way: an in-app PROOF LAB re-runs the verification suite live — the connectivity algorithms cross-checked against independent brute-force references over 1,500 random graphs, and the real solver proving determinism, valid adjacency, the whole-network guarantee and the terminal-routing guarantee across many seeds. Strictly additive: with connectivity off it is byte-for-byte the solver it always was.
